title: Legal Counsel
description:
**Job Title:**
==============


Legal Counsel**Summary:**
============


In this role you will become an integral part of our regional EMEAI Legal Team, supporting a wide range of legal matters from commercial legal advisory to compliance stewardship. You will provide expert legal guidance to Momentive’s commercial teams and functions across Italy and Europe, helping to ensure compliance with local laws and regulations while enabling business objectives. The role requires strong business acumen as well as a solid understanding of company financials and economic principles.**Responsibilities Include:**
=============================

* Provide legal support to regional Sales Teams on customer contracts and commercial legal aspects, including drafting, review and negotiation of customer contracts.
* Provide legal support to regional customer service teams on general commercial and customer\-related legal matters, including customer claims and inquiries.
* Provide legal support to regional and local Procurement Teams, including drafting, review and negotiation of procurement contracts.
* Manage corporate law requirements for local Italian group entities.
* Advise on Italian labor law and partner with local HR team on legal aspects related to HR/employee relations, collective bargaining agreements in Italy.
* Provide legal advice and support on Compliance and regulatory requirements to Momentive’s business operations in Italy, including Data Privacy Compliance.
* Manage internal investigation, legal disputes and litigation in Italy and Europe, coordinating with external counsel as needed with the oversight of senior legal team members.
* Monitor legal and regulatory developments in region and recommend proactive measures.
* Provide legal support in matters related to Legislative Decree 231/2001, including collaboration with the Supervisory Body (OdV).
* Support compliance activities and internal reviews in coordination with BoD, HR, EHS and OdV, as applicable.
* Deliver training and guidance to local and regional teams on legal policies and compliance.
* Collaborate with global legal teams to align regional practices with corporate standards.
* Promote a culture of compliance, ethical conduct, and risk prevention across the organization.


\#LI\-KG1

**Qualifications:**
===================

**The following are** ***required*** **for the role**

* **Education:** University law degree; admission to practice law in Italy.
* **Experience:** 3\-6 years of legal experience in Italy, preferably in a multinational corporate environment or reputable law firm.
* **Skills:**
	+ Strong knowledge of Italian and European commercial and corporate laws.
	+ Expertise in contract negotiation and regulatory compliance.
	+ Ability to manage legal risk across multiple jurisdictions in Europe.
	+ Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
	+ Business acumen and understanding of financial and strategic priorities in global companies.
	+ Excellent Italian and English language skills.

**What We Offer:**
==================


Base Pay Range: €43\.800 \- €64\.240
Compensation for this role will be competitive and aligned with market practices in the respective location and, where applicable, with the provisions of the applicable National Collective Labor Agreement (CCNL), including relevant sector agreements (e.g., the Chemical Industry agreement or, for executive roles, the agreement for Executives of Companies Producing Goods and Services in Italy). Actual pay is determined based on several factors, including but not limited to experience, education, skills, and geographic location. Where applicable, country\-specific salary ranges will be communicated during the hiring process in line with local pay transparency regulations. **If you are interested in this position, we’d ask that you apply! \#BePartoftheSolution**

  

This role may also be eligible for variable incentive compensation. Any such incentives are governed by the terms of the Company’s applicable plans and are based on company performance and/or individual performance.

  

At Momentive, we value your well\-being and offer competitive total rewards and development programs. Our inclusive culture fosters a strong sense of belonging and provides diverse career opportunities to help you unleash your full potential. Together, through innovative problem\-solving and collaboration, we strive to create sustainable solutions that make a meaningful impact.

**About Us:**
=============


Momentive is a premier global advanced materials company with a cutting\-edge focus on silicones and specialty products. We deliver solutions designed to help propel our customer’s products forward—products that have a profound impact on all aspects of life, around the clock and from living rooms to outer space. With every innovation, Momentive creates a more sustainable future. Our vast product portfolio is made up of advanced silicones and specialty solutions that play an essential role in driving performance across a multitude of industries, including agriculture, automotive, aerospace, electronics, energy, healthcare, personal care, consumer products, building and construction, and more.

  



Momentive believes a diverse workforce empowers our people, strengthens our business, and contributes to a sustainable world. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any characteristic protected by law.

  

To be considered for this position candidates are required to submit an application for employment and be of legal working age as defined by local law. An offer may be conditioned upon the successful completion of pre\-employment conditions, as applicable, and subject to applicable laws and regulations.

 **Note to third parties:** Momentive is not seeking or accepting any unsolicited assistance from search and selection firms or employment agencies at this time.

title: Director of Operations - Italy
description:
Led by an experienced management team and supported by a strong investor group, including large and experienced institutions and strategic partners, EdgeConneX offers a dynamic, fast\-paced work environment where we are bringing flexibility, proximity, power, and connectivity to some of the world's key businesses. With major offices in Herndon, Denver, and Amsterdam, we have a global footprint and a unified team of employees committed to providing a premier customer experience and delivering the full spectrum of data center solutions, from core to edge, like no other data center provider can do.



Focused on driving innovation and helping our customers define and deliver their own unique vision for the Edge, at any scale, in any market worldwide, for any requirement, we are building tomorrow's data center infrastructure, today for some of the world's most demanding Network, Content, and Cloud customers.


The Director of Operations \- Italy, is EdgeConneX's regional operations leader will be accountable for the 24×7×365 performance of all data centres across Italy. This role is responsible for translating corporate objectives into executable regional plans, leading Campus Managers and site teams through established policies and standards, and driving continuous improvement in operational procedures and practices. The Director will oversee regional budget execution, shapes workforce priorities, and partners closely with cross\-functional stakeholders including, Sales, Engineering, PMO, and Customer teams.



This role requires onsite training and onboarding at one or more EdgeConneX locations. Travel is expected to be significant during the first six months.


**Primary Responsibilities**


* Own overall operational performance across all Italian data centre sites, including uptime, SLA compliance, PUE, safety, and customer satisfaction, while delivering results in line with company policies and targets.
* Lead Campus Managers across Italy, managing team performance, setting objectives, and guiding workforce planning and development within established HR frameworks.
* Translate objective\-based assignments from the VP of Operations into effective resource plans, schedules, and operational methods required to achieve defined goals.
* Recommend and implement changes to regional operational policies, procedures, and standards, ensuring consistency across all sites.
* Manage execution of the regional operating budget, monitor costs, and identify operational expenditure efficiencies without compromising uptime or service quality.
* Oversee the regional vendor and contractor landscape, ensuring contractual SLA and KPI commitments are met.
* Oversee the preventive maintenance programme, change management, incident management, and disaster recovery planning across the Italian portfolio.
* Provide operational leadership and input into new site commissioning, capacity expansions, and infrastructure upgrades across the region.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers and internal stakeholders, acting as the senior escalation point for regional operational issues.
* Partner with cross\-functional teams including Sales, Finance, HR, and PMO to align operational readiness with business growth objectives.
* Maintain active relationships with local regulatory authorities to ensure timely compliance with statutory notifications, reporting obligations, and inspections, and serve as the regional point of contact for regulatory enquiries and audits.
* Report regional KPIs and business performance to the VP of Operations, preparing business reviews and recommendations to support decision\-making.
* Travel as required


**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**


* Proven leader and confident decision\-maker whose judgement carries significant business impact.
* Knowledge of multi\-site, cross\-border operational leadership practices, including team development through subordinate managers.
* Knowledge of data centre operational standards, best practices, and 24x7 critical infrastructure management.
* Ability to translate corporate objectives into operational plans, prioritise work, and drive accountability for execution.
* Skill in budget execution, cost monitoring, and vendor contract management.
* Strong communication and influencing skills, with the ability to engage effectively across functions and with customers and external stakeholders.
* Ability to analyse operational data, apply sound judgement, and identify opportunities for improvement.
* Ability to make confident, high\-impact decisions in a complex operational environment.
* Fluency in English and proficiency in Italian is required.


**Experience \& Education**


* Minimum 12 years' experience in data centre operations, critical facilities engineering, or a related technical leadership field.
* Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related discipline preferred; equivalent professional experience considered.
* Experience commissioning new data centres and transitioning sites from construction to live operations.
* Demonstrated experience in commissioning new data centre facilities and transitioning sites from construction to live operations.
* Familiarity with Italian regulatory and safety frameworks an advantage.


*The starting salary will be no less than €68,880 gross per year and the final offer will be fine tuned based on gender neutral criteria of candidates, including but not limited to competencies, responsibilities, effort and work conditions.*


EdgeConneX believes inclusion – of thought, backgrounds and experiences – affects all that we do, from our employees to the solutions we deliver. Our goal is to create an environment where embracing differences helps deepen the lives and work experience of our employees, enhances our innovation and creativity, and enriches our involvement in our communities. EdgeConneX is an equal employment opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin or ancestry, physical or mental disability, as well as any other category protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws.



EdgeConneX offers a competitive benefits package.

title: Senior Consultant - AI Strategy & Governance
description:
At Capgemini Invent, we believe difference drives change. As inventive transformation consultants, we blend our strategic, creative and scientific capabilities, collaborating closely with clients to deliver cutting\-edge solutions. Join us to drive transformation tailored to our client's challenges of today and tomorrow. Informed and validated by science and data. Superpowered by creativity and design. All underpinned by technology created with purpose.


**YOUR ROLE**
  

As part of our Enterprise and Data Analytics practice at Capgemini Invent, you will help organisations unlock the value of AI by defining strategic roadmaps, establishing governance frameworks, and enabling responsible AI adoption at scale. Working closely with C\-suite executives and business leaders, you will support transformation initiatives that balance innovation, business value, risk management, and regulatory compliance. In this role you will play a key role in:
  

* Defining AI and GenAI strategies, operating models, and transformation roadmaps that are closely aligned with clients' business objectives and long\-term vision.
* Designing and implementing AI Governance and Responsible AI frameworks, ensuring compliance, transparency, risk management, and ethical AI adoption across the organisation.
* Advising senior executives on AI investment priorities, use case portfolios, and value realisation opportunities, translating complex topics into clear and actionable business decisions.
* Overseeing complex consulting engagements, coordinating multidisciplinary teams across strategy, technology, data, and change management to deliver measurable client impact.
* Supporting clients in scaling AI capabilities through governance structures, Centres of Excellence, and enterprise\-wide adoption approaches that drive sustainable transformation.
* Helping shape and expand our AI Strategy \& Governance practice through active contributions to proposals, thought leadership publications, and client development initiatives.

**YOUR PROFILE**
  

* 5\+ years of experience in Strategy Consulting, Digital Transformation, Data \& AI, or Technology Advisory, with a proven track record of delivering initiatives with measurable business impact.
* Strong understanding of AI, Generative AI, and Agentic AI, combined with solid knowledge of the governance, risk, and compliance considerations associated with their adoption at scale.
* Demonstrated experience designing operating models, governance frameworks, target operating organisations, or enterprise transformation roadmaps in complex, multi\-stakeholder environments.
* A proven ability to engage and influence senior stakeholders, including C\-level executives, building trusted relationships and translating complex technical topics into clear business outcomes.
* A collaborative and inclusive working style, with the ability to coordinate and inspire multidisciplinary teams while fostering a culture of knowledge sharing and continuous learning.
* A proactive and innovative mindset, excellent communication skills, and a genuine passion for shaping the future of AI\-enabled organisations in an ethical and responsible way.
* We are looking for experienced consultants who combine strategic thinking, leadership capabilities, and a strong understanding of AI technologies and governance challenges. If you are passionate about shaping the future of AI\-enabled organisations and thrive in a collaborative, fast\-paced environment, we'd love to hear from you.

*Note: Fluent Italian and English required.*
**WHAT YOU'LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING HERE**
  

* We recognize the importance of flexible work solutions to support you. Whether it’s remote work, flexible hours, or both, you’ll find an environment that promotes work\-life balance;
* At the heart of our mission is your career, with professional development paths designed to help you explore a world of opportunities;.
* We are committed to fostering social responsibility by creating an inclusive workplace where diverse talents are valued. Diversity is a cornerstone of our corporate culture, enriching our work through different experiences, cultures, ethnicities, communication styles, and behaviors;
* You will play a key role in driving the definition of end to end technology strategies and digital transformations for our clients, helping to design IT roadmaps, orchestrate complex programs, and develop innovation visions that bring together business, technology, and change management.

**Location**: Rome or Milan, Italy (with flexibility for hybrid work and client travel)
  

**Type of contract**: Permanent employment contract
  

**Job Classification**: Metalworking Industry National Collective Labour Agreement (CCNL Metalmeccanico) – Level B3
  

**Position**: Consultant/Senior Consultant
  

**Gross Annual Salary (RAL)**: from €36,907 to €61,700


The salary range indicated has been defined in compliance with European and national legislation on pay transparency.
  

The minimum value of the range corresponds to the minimum salary treatment established by the applicable National Collective Labour Agreement (CCNL) for the level and position indicated above and does not necessarily represent the starting salary. The compensation offered at the conclusion of the selection process will be determined based on the candidate’s skills, professional experience, level of seniority, and the specific characteristics of the selected profile, in line with the company’s compensation and job classification system, and in accordance with objective and gender\-neutral criteria.

  

  

**CAPGEMINI**
  

Capgemini is an AI\-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of over 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end\-to\-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2025 global revenues of €22\.5 billion.
  

Make it real. \| www.capgemini.com
  

Apply Now!
  

The job offer is intended for both sexes in compliance with Legislative Decree 198/2006\. Additionally, we pay attention and sensitivity to future resources belonging to protected categories, pursuant to articles 1 and 18 of Law 68/99\. Personal data will be processed in accordance with the provisions contained in EU Regulation 679/2016

title: EHS Consultant
description:
**Job Description** **Consultant \- EHS**


Milan, Italy


Do you want to push the boundaries of your profession and develop your excellence in an open, collaborative and empowering culture? Are you motivated by creating sustainable change that benefits society and nature? Are you a curious and open\-minded person?


If this sounds like you, or you’re curious to learn more, then this role could be the perfect opportunity. Join our Environment and Health Department as our new EHS consultant and work with us to close the gap to a sustainable future.

**Your new role**


As our new **Senior EHS Consultant**, you will support the delivery of Environmental, Health \& Safety (EHS) Due Diligence and Compliance projects for private equity, corporate and financial clients across a variety of industrial sectors. You will work closely with Project Managers and multidisciplinary teams to assess EHS risks and liabilities, conduct compliance reviews and audits, and support transaction\-related advisory services throughout the M\&A lifecycle.


The role requires a strong understanding of both environmental and occupational health \& safety regulations, together with the ability to identify material EHS risks, evaluate compliance status, and communicate findings effectively to clients and stakeholders.


Candidates can work in fully hybrid working pattern from our Rome or Milan office in line with our flexible working policy.

 **Your key responsibilities will be:**

* Conduct Environmental, Health \& Safety Due Diligence assessments in support of mergers, acquisitions, divestments and financing transactions.
* Perform EHS compliance audits and site assessments across a wide range of industrial and commercial facilities.
* Review environmental permitting, regulatory compliance obligations, and management systems.
* Evaluate occupational health \& safety programs, risk assessments, contractor management processes, and related documentation.
* Support the identification, quantification, and prioritization of EHS risks, liabilities and improvement opportunities.
* Prepare high\-quality technical reports, proposals and client deliverables in Italian and English.
* Participate in site visits, client meetings, presentations and interactions with external stakeholders, including legal advisors, investors, operators and regulators.
* Contribute to the continuous development of the team.
* Support business development activities, proposal preparation and client relationship management.

**Your new team**


You will be part of part of our successful team composed by engineers, geologists, environmental scientists, chemists, biologists, etc. working together in an international network for a variety of projects regarding mainly contaminated site management, expert services, permitting, due diligence, impact assessment, water treatment, health and safety, climate risk and sustainability. You will contribute to a sustainable future in an open, collaborative and empowering culture.

 **About you**


From the moment you start at Ramboll, we will support your personal and professional development so that you can continue to grow with the company. Whilst we look forward to supporting your continued learning and development, for this role we have identified some qualifications, skills, and capabilities that will set you up for success.


These include:

* A Master's degree in Environmental Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Health \& Safety Engineering, Environmental Sciences, Geology, or a related discipline.
* At least **5 years of professional experience** in EHS consulting, compliance, auditing, due diligence or related advisory services.
* Demonstrated experience in conducting EHS compliance audits and site assessments.
* Sound knowledge of Italian environmental and occupational health \& safety legislation, including Legislative Decree 152/2006 and Legislative Decree 81/2008\.
* Experience supporting EHS Due Diligence projects within the M\&A, transaction advisory or investment environment.
* Strong analytical, report\-writing and project execution skills.
* Excellent communication skills and the ability to engage effectively with clients and multidisciplinary stakeholders.
* Fluency in Italian and English, both written and spoken.


The following would be considered an advantage:

* Previous experience within an environmental, engineering or sustainability consulting firm.
* Auditor qualifications (e.g. ISO 14001 and/or ISO 45001 Lead Auditor).
* RSPP qualification or other relevant EHS certifications.
* Experience with ESG assessments, sustainability due diligence or environmental liability evaluations.
* Knowledge of additional European languages.

  

What we can offer you

* Flexible work environment
* Remote working up to 3\-4 days per week
* Lunch vouchers
* Investment in your development
* Leaders you can count on, guided by our Leadership Principles
* Never be short of inspiration from colleagues, clients, and projects

 **Work at the heart of sustainable change**


Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. We believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. At Ramboll, our core strength is our people, and our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act. Being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. We embrace an inclusive mindset that looks for fresh, diverse, and innovative perspectives. We respect, embrace, and invite diversity in all forms to actively cultivate an environment where everyone can flourish and realise their full potential.

 **Ready to join us?**


Please submit your application. Be sure to include all relevant documents including your CV, cover letter, etc.  

  

The salary range for this position is 34 000 to 45 000 EUR per year
company_description: Ramboll is a global engineering, architecture, and consultancy company with more than 18,000 experts who create sustainable solutions for clients all over the world.

Posted 09\.07\.2026

At Bryden Wood, we are expanding our team in Milan to work on large scale projects for the biggest names in the Data Centre and Pharmaceutical sectors.
  
  

We are looking for an Associate Architect with site experience, to lead the technical delivery of our Data Centre and Pharmaceutical projects. This is your chance to work in a truly international environment, fully integrated with our UK HQ, while enjoying benefits that set a new standard for the Italian market.
  
  

The role
  
  

You will be one of the Architectural leads of our projects in Italy as well as key member on other international projects, working closely with all the other disciplines (Structures, Civils, MEP and Sustainability) and clients to deliver innovative designs.
  
  

* Lead the Architectural design for all project stages (from Concept to Construction) including carrying out regulatory compliance check, concept development, Project coordination, H\&S, Risk management and project delivery.
* Supervise and coordinate multidisciplinary teams of Architects and Engineers, assigning tasks, providing technical guidance, and ensuring team cohesion toward project goals.
* Ensure full compliance with Italian regulations.
* Produce, coordinate and possibly lead official submissions to local authorities.
* Manage project coordination, QA/QC procedures, and risk management.
* Mentor junior Architects and liaise with senior management on project escalations.


What we need
  
  

* Degree in Architecture or equivalent.
* Must\-Have: Deep knowledge of Italian regulations and site experience during construction stages, liaising with General Contractors and some experience with Direzione dei Lavori
* Minimum of 8 years of professional experience, preferable with previous experience in Data Centre or Pharmaceutical projects in Italy.
* Experience in producing, delivering and possibly signing off official submissions to local authorities.
* Language: Full professional fluency in spoken and written Italian and English.
* Project management: Proven experience in project management would be beneficial.
* Revit \& Navisworks: Competent in the use of Revit and Navisworks


Why join us?
  
  

**Our benefits package is designed to be the best in the sector:**
  
  

* Full\-time employment with CCNL Commercio. The salary will be Competitive and based on experience.
* 9\-Day Fortnight: Enjoy one Friday off every two weeks \- no extra hours required, and no change to your salary.
* Hybrid working: Hybrid working arrangement with a 2 days working from home policy.
* Global Impact: Work on Data Centres and Large\-Scale Pharmaceutical facilities across Europe.
* Paid Professional Membership: We’ve got your membership covered.
* Career Progression: Regular performance reviews to support your growth.
* Learning Opportunities: Regular CPDs to keep you sharp.
* Fun \& Social Events: Team lunches, office breakfasts and snacks, outings and company socials to keep things lively.

title: Risk Data Aggregation & Risk Reporting Associate - Milano [OTS]
description:
Trasforma le tue aspirazioni professionali in una storia di successo, entra in PwC come:


Risk Data Aggregation \& Risk Reporting Associate

**Your Perspectives:**


Il team Risk Capital \& Reporting affianca i clienti finanziari nella gestione integrata dei rischi e del capitale, combinando competenze quantitative, regolamentari e di reporting, con focus su capital management, framework di Enterprise Risk, analisi degli impatti normativi sul business e sviluppo di soluzioni per il monitoraggio e la misurazione dei rischi. In particolare, all’interno del nostro team prenderai parte a progetti di disegno e realizzazione di framework di misurazione, gestione e reporting dei rischi per Gruppi Bancari, Assicurativi ed Enti attivi nell’Asset \& Wealth Management, lavorando a stretto contatto con professionisti esperti sul cliente, sia on\-site sia da remoto. Entra nel nostro team e avrai l’opportunità di:

* Supportare attività di analisi normativa in ambito regolamentare (es. framework Basilea), contribuendo a progetti di Regulatory Reporting e Regulatory Strategy.
* Partecipare a progetti di trasformazione in ambito Risk, supportando i clienti nella gestione di iniziative complesse di Change Management.
* Contribuire alla definizione di modelli di Governance e al miglioramento dei processi organizzativi.
* Supportare il coordinamento e l’avanzamento progettuale attraverso un approccio strutturato e flessibile di Project Management.
 **Your****unique****strengths**

* Hai conseguito una laurea con un background in ambito finanziario, economico\-statistico o ingegneristico.
* Possiedi comprovata esperienza da 1 a 3 anni in Aziende Leader e/o in primarie Società di consulenza, con riferimento al Framework di risk reporting (RDARR/BCBS239\) e/o con conoscenza base dei principi Data Governance.
* Hai un'ottima conoscenza del Pacchetto Office (Excel, Power Point, ...).
* Hai una conoscenza base di linguaggi di programmazione (es. SAS /Python).
* Parli e scrivi in inglese fluentemente.
* Sei unə team worker e possiedi buone capacità relazionali e comunicative.
 **Set****your****expectation****higher**


PwC ti sorprenderà con un’opportunità di carriera che ti farà sentire al centro della società, dell’innovazione e del cambiamento. Il talento e la curiosità avranno tutto lo spazio che meritano e contribuirai a garantire la qualità dei servizi che offriamo al nostro interno. In questo percorso di crescita ti supporterà un team esperto e avrai una formazione continua e su misura grazie con la quale potrai mettere le tue competenze a disposizione della clientela interna.

 **Feel****at****home**


Ci prendiamo cura delle nostre persone, abbracciando le diversità e creando valore per la clientela, le persone e la comunità, nel rispetto dell'ambiente.


GIVE YOUR LIFE A DIFFERENT PERSPECTIVE


Per questa opportunità è previsto un inserimento con contratto al:  

  

* 3° livello CCNL Commercio (28\.000€\-30\.000€) per candidati senza esperienza o esperienza minima
* 2° livello CCNL Commercio (31\.500€\-32\.500€) per candidati con comprovata esperienza nel ruolo

  

L'offerta finale sarà definita anche in base alla valutazione delle tue competenze ed esperienze pregresse.  

  

Entrare in PwC significa anche poter contare su un ecosistema di iniziative e benefit pensati per supportare benessere, crescita e work\-life integration.  

Il pacchetto include:  

* Buoni pasto del valore di 8€

* Piano welfare dedicato
* Programmi e iniziative di wellbeing
* Convenzioni

title: VP, Regional RX (Prescription Medicines) Commercial Operations Lead
description:
**.buttontext6ff3b9177bc58513 a{ border:** 1px solid transparent; } .buttontext6ff3b9177bc58513 a: focus{ border: 1px dashed \#000000 !important; outline: none !important; }
  
**Publish Date:** Jul 14, 2026
  
  

Milano, IT
  
  

**\#job\-location.job\-location\-inline { display:** inline; }
  
VP, Regional RX (Prescription Medicines) Commercial Operations Lead
  
  

Alfasigma is a privately held global healthcare company founded over 75 years ago in Italy, where it remains headquartered today in Bologna and Milan. The Group operates in over 100 markets spanning Europe, North and South America, Asia, and Africa.
  
  

Over three years ago, we embarked on a transformative journey to become a truly global innovative healthcare company with a leading focus on Gastrointestinal (GI) health. We have experienced remarkable growth, nearly doubled our revenues, expanding geographically, and integrating several key acquisitions. We have also evolved our portfolio, expanded into rare and specialty segments, and strengthened our pipeline and people's capabilities.
  
  

But our journey is far from complete! In fact, this is just the beginning of a bold new chapter in Alfasigma's history. We are committed to advancing innovation and leveraging our deep expertise to provide better health and a better quality of life for patients and consumers worldwide and expand our reach across diverse markets.
  
  

Scope of the Role
  
  

Within the Global Commercial Operations \& Execution Team, the Regional RX Commercial Operations Lead is accountable for accelerating the global development and in\-market performance of the Prescription Medicines portfolio, working in close partnership with country organizations.
  
  

The role acts as a senior Global–Local commercial leader, ensuring alignment with global brand and franchise strategies for strategic products, while enabling superior country planning, launch execution and sustained in\-market performance.
  
  

Covering established and innovative products across primary care, specialty care, hospital and rare disease segments, the role requires a deep understanding of local market dynamics, regulatory and market access environments, and complex stakeholder ecosystems, and plays a critical role in building long\-term commercial capabilities across RX markets.
  
  

What will you do
  
  

Provide global commercial leadership to RX markets, working closely with Country General Managers and local RX leadership teams to drive performance, accountability and execution excellence across the portfolio
  
Act as a senior strategic partner to country organizations, supporting and challenging country business plans, launch strategies and key commercial initiatives to ensure alignment with global priorities and local market realities
  
Serve as the primary two\-way Global–Local interface, translating global franchise, brand and go\-to\-market strategies into integrated, executable country plans, while channeling structured market insights, risks and opportunities back to Global teams
  
Oversee ongoing execution and performance management across the RX portfolio, monitoring progress against agreed country plans, driving corrective actions where needed and ensuring disciplined, structured performance management across markets.
  
Support launch excellence for strategic RX assets across countries, in line with global strategies, by providing senior oversight and hands\-on support on launch readiness, sequencing, execution quality and post\-launch optimization.
  
Develop a deep, ground\-level understanding of market dynamics, including competitive landscape, treatment paradigms, pricing \& reimbursement environments and stakeholder needs (HCPs, payers, institutions), leveraging country insights to inform portfolio and lifecycle decisions
  
Identify and address commercial capability gaps across countries, acting as an enabler for RX markets by developing targeted capability\-building initiatives in partnership with Global Capability lead
  
Represent country and regional needs towards Global Franchises and Corporate functions, influencing global strategy, investment prioritization and operating model choices based on real\-world execution experience
  
Partner with the Global Non\-Strategic Portfolio PMO to identify, assess and prioritize opportunities within the non\-strategic RX portfolio, supporting robust business case development with strong country input
  
Champion innovation and continuous improvement, promoting best practice sharing across countries and driving initiatives to enhance commercial effectiveness, launch excellence and operational efficiency
  
  

Who are you
  
  

Professional Experience
  
  

Minimum 12–15 years of progressive leadership experience within the pharmaceutical RX environment
  
Proven success in senior commercial leadership roles (e.g. Business Unit Head, Country Commercial Head, Global or Regional Commercial Lead), with strong exposure to country\-level execution
  
Demonstrated executive\-level P\&L ownership, including strategic planning, performance delivery and resource prioritization
  
Strong track record of multiple successful RX launches, ideally across different markets and therapeutic areas
  
Solid experience operating in global matrix organizations, with the ability to influence and lead country teams without direct reporting lines
  
  

Education
  
  

Bachelor’s degree in Pharmacy, Life Sciences, Business or related disciplines
  
MBA or equivalent executive education strongly preferred
  
  

Key Skills \& Leadership Capabilities
  
  

Deep understanding of RX commercial models and country\-level execution dynamics, including regulatory frameworks, pricing \& reimbursement and access complexity
  
Ability to combine global strategic thinking with strong local market orientation
  
Proven capability to lead, influence and align country leadership teams across geographies and cultures
  
Excellent executive communication and stakeholder management skills, spanning Global Franchises, Local Leadership Teams and Corporate functions
  
Strong financial and analytical acumen, leveraging qualitative and quantitative insights to guide decisions
  
Expertise in brand strategy implementation, launch governance and execution monitoring
  
Demonstrated ability to build sustainable commercial capabilities across markets
  
  

**Why Join Alfasigma:**  

At Alfasigma, we foster a culture where the courage to innovate is key to our success.
  
We offer a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and extensive opportunities for professional growth and development.
  
  

Our commitment to people and patients is at the heart of everything we do. We value diversity and welcome individuals with unique perspectives and experiences. We believe that open\-mindedness, collaboration, and a shared passion for innovation are essential to achieving meaningful progress.
  
  

Join Alfasigma and become part of a forward\-thinking team dedicated to shaping the future of the pharmaceutical industry.

title: Sales Pharmacy - Internship
description:
Welcome to Haleon. We’re a purpose\-driven, world\-class consumer company putting everyday health in the hands of millions. In just three years since our launch, we’ve grown, evolved and are now entering an exciting new chapter – one filled with bold ambitions and enormous opportunity.
  

  

Our trusted portfolio of brands – including Sensodyne®, Panadol®, Advil®, Voltaren®, Theraflu®, Otrivin®, and Centrum® – lead in resilient and growing categories. What sets us apart is our unique blend of deep human understanding and trusted science.
  

  

Now it’s time to fully realise the full potential of our business and our people. We do this through our Win as One strategy. It puts our purpose – to deliver better everyday health with humanity – at the heart of everything we do. It unites us, inspires us, and challenges us to be better every day, driven by our agile, performance\-focused culture.


**Sales Pharmacy – Internship**

**About the role**

We are looking for a qualified sales intern to assist in various stages of the sales funnel, including creating awareness of new offerings, generating leads, and retaining customers especially in the Indirect Pharmacy channel, chains and e\-Pharmacies.


The ideal intern will be a great fit for our energetic team: working on increasingly challenging and engaging real\-world projects, gaining hands\-on experience in each stage of the sales funnel, attending meetings, events, and other networking opportunities.


The intern hired for this position will help create, identify and track sales leads, generate sales reporting, evaluate success, trends and failures of sales activities, assist with sales forecasting, strategies and tactics.


**Key responsibilities:**

* Learn basic principle of Key Account Management
* Learn and contribute to the development of sales concepts and go\-to\-market strategies.
* Support Sales team in monitoring and updating prior clients’ lists.

Good level of English

2026\-08\-07

----------------------

title: Global Cybersecurity Operations Manager
description:
**Job Description****Who we are**


Amplifon is an Italian multinational company and the global leader in hearing care solutions and services. More than 20,000 professionals every day, in a network of 9,500 points of sale across 24 countries, give back the joy of hearing, feeling and living to thousands of people across the world.


We believe people are the most important component of our success, and that it is only through strong investment in talent, continuous development, support and recognition that our people can exceed every limit and build a fulfilling career. Aligned to our Winning Workplace, we offer a hybrid working policy allowing employees to work 9 days/month remotely.

**Role Purpose**


Amplifon is looking for a **Global Security Operations Manager** to lead the day\-to\-day execution of the Group's Security Operations, ensuring effective monitoring, detection, response and continuous improvement of cybersecurity capabilities across a multinational environment.


Reporting to the **Group Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)**, the Global Security Operations Manager is responsible for coordinating internal cybersecurity specialists and managed security partners (SOC/MDR, NDR, endpoint protection, incident response providers and specialist vendors), ensuring efficient and resilient security operations on a global scale.


The role combines operational leadership with strong technical expertise, driving continuous improvement across security monitoring, incident response, vulnerability management and security platform operations while operating within the Group's governance and compliance framework.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Security Operations**

  

* Manage the daily execution of the Group Security Operations across endpoint, network, identity and cloud environments.
* Coordinate the Security Operations Center (SOC) and Managed Detection \& Response (MDR) services, ensuring service quality and adherence to agreed SLAs.
* Improve detection capabilities by developing and maintaining SIEM use cases, playbooks and security automation initiatives.
* Monitor operational performance and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.

  

**Incident Response \& Cyber Resilience**  

* Coordinate the end\-to\-end incident response process, including preparation, detection, containment, eradication, recovery and post\-incident reviews.
* Manage major security incidents in collaboration with internal stakeholders and external incident response partners.
* Drive remediation activities and support initiatives aimed at strengthening cyber resilience and operational readiness.

  

**Vulnerability \& Threat Management**  

* Manage the vulnerability management lifecycle, including identification, prioritization, remediation tracking and validation.
* Support threat intelligence activities and exposure management initiatives to prioritize security risks.
* Coordinate penetration testing and security assessment activities with infrastructure and application teams.

  

**Security Engineering \& Platform Operations**  

* Oversee the operational management and continuous improvement of the Group's security technologies, including SIEM, SOAR, EDR/XDR, NDR, email security, firewalls, MFA and privileged access management.
* Collaborate with infrastructure and cloud teams to implement secure configurations and security\-by\-design principles.

  

**Vendor \& Team Coordination**  

* Coordinate a team of security specialists, supporting their development and ensuring effective collaboration across countries.
* Manage relationships with managed security service providers and specialist vendors, monitoring service performance, escalations and operational deliverables.

  

**Reporting \& Planning**  

* Monitor operational KPIs, SLAs and security metrics, providing regular reporting to the Group CISO.
* Support budget planning, capacity planning and continuous improvement initiatives within the Security Operations function.

  

**Governance, Risk \& Compliance**  

* Ensure Security Operations activities comply with the Group Information Security Management System (ISMS), ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2 and applicable regulations.
* Support internal and external audits and contribute to maintaining compliance with security policies and standards.

  

**Candidate Profile****Experience**

  

* 6\-8 years of experience in cybersecurity, including significant experience in Security Operations, SOC or Incident Response environments.
* Previous experience managing security operations teams or coordinating technical cybersecurity professionals.
* Experience working with Managed Security Service Providers (MSSPs) and external security vendors.
* Experience handling cybersecurity incidents and coordinating response activities.
* Exposure to multinational and complex enterprise environments is preferred.

  

**Technical Skills**  

* Strong knowledge of Security Operations technologies, including SIEM/SOAR, EDR/XDR, vulnerability management, threat intelligence and incident response.
* Good understanding of enterprise infrastructure, networking, cloud platforms (Azure, AWS and/or GCP) and identity security.
* Familiarity with security frameworks and standards including ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2, GDPR and NIST.

  

**Leadership \& Soft Skills**  

* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Ability to prioritize activities and manage multiple initiatives in a fast\-paced environment.
* Analytical mindset with a continuous improvement approach.
* Collaborative leadership style and ability to work across international teams.

  

**Education \& Certifications**  

* Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Cybersecurity or equivalent experience.
* Professional certifications such as CISSP, CISM, GCIH or similar are considered a plus.
* Fluency in English is mandatory; Italian is a plus.

**What We Offer**

  

* The opportunity to contribute to the security operations of a leading global company operating across 24 countries.
* Close collaboration with the Group CISO and international technology teams.

**Location**: HQ Milan, Italy (Hybrid \- allowing employees to work 6 days per month remotely)


**Compensation \& Benefits**


**Applicable Collective Agreement**: CCNL Commercio (Terziario, Distribuzione e Servizi)


**Contractual Level**:Quadro

**Contract Type**: Permanent \- Full Time


The reference **Gross Annual Salary** is approximately in the range of EUR 70\.000 \- 92\.000 . The actual compensation will be commensurate with the candidate's experience and specific skills and knowledge and also to the educational background.


The position is also eligible for an annual incentive scheme based on Company and individual performance KPIs, with a payout whose target is equal to 15% of the annual gross salary, in line with Company policies.


**Benefits**  

  



* **€1,700 Annual Welfare plan** for your personal well\-being on a wide range of services
* **Health Insurance: QUAS** healthcare coverage and an additional company integrative insurance \+ Accident Insurance
* **Supplementary pension scheme:**option to enrol in the Fondo Fon.Te with employer contributions
* **Office Perks:**On\-site **canteen**and free **company parking**
* **BeWellProgram:**Free access to learning platforms and training programs. We invest in your growth through ongoing learning opportunities, while also offering special corporate discounts, dedicated services for you and your loved ones, and initiatives focused on your wellbeing and physical health

**Equal Opportunities Statement**


Amplifon is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing a diverse and equitable workforce environment. We believe that through valuing our uniqueness and respecting our differences, we can achieve more, and that diversity adds to our culture. We encourage applications from all genders, corners of the world and individual backgrounds.
